title: zyrphelis angolensis
intro: Zyrphelis angolensis, also known as Angola's firebush, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, native to Southern Africa. Traditionally, Zyrphelis angolensis has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of limonene and other volatile compounds. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Zyrphelis angolensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
